name: Shrimp Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop shrimp roughly and set aside.
2. In the top of a double boiler over boiling water, melt butter.
3. Add flour, mustard, and tarragon to the melted butter. Cook, stirring constantly for about 5 minutes until the mixture is thick.
4. Add milk all at once and stir until a thick sauce is formed. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
5. Beat egg yolks. Gradually beat them into the sauce. Stir in lemon juice and grated lemon peel.
6. Butter a 1-quart souffle dish and dust well with grated cheese.
7. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
8. Beat egg whites until stiff but not dry.
9. Gently stir shrimp into the sauce, then fold in egg whites using as few strokes as possible. Do not overmix.
10. Pour the mixture into the souffle dish and bake for about 35 minutes, until the souffle is puffed, browned, and fairly firm. Decorate with whole cooked shrimp if desired.
11. Serve immediately. Serves 4 to 6.
